checklist.question: Did receivables grow materially faster than revenue across consecutive periods?

evidence.quote: AccountsReceivableNet=1616382000 (2011-12-31) vs AccountsReceivableNet=1356553000 (2010-12-31): +19.2% growth
Revenues=8299300000 (FY2011) vs Revenues=7562534000 (FY2010): +9.7% growth
AccountsReceivableNet=1879700000 (2012-12-31) vs AccountsReceivableNet=1616382000 (2011-12-31): +16.3% growth
Revenues=9024900000 (FY2012) vs Revenues=8299300000 (FY2011): +8.7% growth
AccountsReceivableNet=1512600000 (2013-12-31), a -19.5% decline vs AccountsReceivableNet=1879700000 (2012-12-31), while Revenues=10771900000 (FY2013) grew +19.3% vs Revenues=9024900000 (FY2012)

checklist.question: Did non-current 'soft' assets (intangibles, other assets, capitalized items) grow materially faster than the business overall?

evidence.quote: IntangibleAssetsNetExcludingGoodwill=4030200000 (2012-12-31) vs IntangibleAssetsNetExcludingGoodwill=2562234000 (2011-12-31): +57.3% growth
Revenues grew only +8.7% over the same period (Revenues=9024900000 FY2012 vs Revenues=8299300000 FY2011)

checklist.question: Is there a persistent divergence between net income and operating cash flow?

evidence.quote: NetIncomeLoss=-126022000 (FY2009) vs NetCashProvidedByUsedInOperatingActivities=1693314000 (FY2009)
evidence.location: Income statement & cash flow statement, FY2009 10-K

evidence.quote: NetIncomeLoss=-48044000 (FY2010) vs NetCashProvidedByUsedInOperatingActivities=2208679000 (FY2010)
evidence.location: Income statement & cash flow statement, FY2010 10-K

evidence.quote: ProfitLoss=346200000 (FY2013) vs NetCashProvidedByUsedInOperatingActivitiesContinuingOperations=3589600000 (FY2013)
evidence.location: Income statement & cash flow statement, FY2013 10-K/A

checklist.question: Do inventory levels or gross margins show trends inconsistent with revenue?

evidence.quote: InventoryNet=92300000 (2013-12-31) vs InventoryNet=105700000 (2012-12-31), a relatively immaterial balance (<1% of revenue) with no COGS/gross-margin line items provided

checklist.question: Is the earnings trajectory unusually smooth relative to the volatility of revenue and cash flow?

evidence.quote: Quarterly NetIncomeLossAvailableToCommonStockholdersBasic swung from -56300000 (Q1 2012) to 87900000 (Q2 2012) to 243800000 (Q3 2012) to -36800000 (Q4 2012)
Quarterly pattern repeats in 2013: 15800000 (Q1) to 120800000 (Q2) to 210200000 (Q3) to -600000 (Q4)

checklist.question: Did liability, allowance, or reserve balances decline (or fail to grow) despite growing business activity?

evidence.quote: Liabilities=13904978000 (2009-12-31) grew to Liabilities=21817200000 (2013-12-31), broadly tracking Assets and Revenue growth
No separate allowance-for-doubtful-accounts, self-insurance reserve, or restructuring reserve concept was provided in the dataset, limiting granular reserve-adequacy testing

checklist.question: Does the filing chronology show irregularities (late filings/NT forms, amendments, unusual 8-K frequency)?

evidence.quote: NT 10-K filed 2009-03-03, same day as 10-K filed 2009-03-03
10-K filed 2010-02-26 followed almost immediately by 10-K/A filed 2010-03-01
NT 10-K filed 2013-03-04, same day as 10-K filed 2013-03-04

NT 10-K filed 2014-03-03, 10-K filed 2014-03-19 (using extension), then 10-K/A filed the very next day, 2014-03-20, restating FY2011-FY2013 balance sheet and income data (e.g., Goodwill, Intangibles, Assets, Liabilities all materially revised)

checklist.question: Is the provided data sufficient in coverage and length for the above assessments?

evidence.quote: Data spans FY2007 through FY2013 with quarterly granularity across ~16 filings (10-Ks, 10-Qs, 10-K/A) for key concepts (Revenues, NetIncomeLoss, Assets, Liabilities, AccountsReceivableNet, Goodwill, Intangibles, Cash, Debt)
Gaps exist for granular items such as allowance for doubtful accounts, self-insurance reserves, COGS/gross margin, and some quarterly NetIncomeLoss figures for 2013-2014, limiting depth on CL4/CL6

mechanism_hypotheses.accounting_treatment: The filing of a 10-K/A only one day after the original FY2013 10-K (10-K filed 2014-03-19, 10-K/A filed 2014-03-20, accession 0001364479-14-000008), which materially revised FY2011-FY2013 balance-sheet figures (e.g., Liabilities=20778100000 (2012-12-31) and Liabilities=21817200000 (2013-12-31) reported only in the 10-K/A, with no matching original-10-K figures for these dates in the underlying dataset), is consistent with a restatement correcting previously understated liabilities/reserves and related overstated net assets or capitalized costs (e.g., self-insurance liabilities, capitalized non-fleet costs, or purchase-accounting adjustments tied to the Dollar Thrifty acquisition).

rationale_evidence.quote: 10-K filed 2014-03-19 followed one day later by 10-K/A filed 2014-03-20 containing revised Assets=23264300000 (2012-12-31), Assets=24588400000 (2013-12-31), Liabilities=20778100000 (2012-12-31), Liabilities=21817200000 (2013-12-31)

mechanism_hypotheses.accounting_treatment: Receivables grew substantially faster than revenue in FY2011 (+19.2% vs +9.7%) and FY2012 (+16.3% vs +8.7%), followed by a sharp reversal in FY2013 (receivables -19.5% while revenue +19.3%). This pattern is consistent with delayed recognition of bad-debt/allowance charges (potentially in the Donlen fleet-leasing or vehicle-licensee receivables) that built up in 2011-2012 and were subsequently written down/reserved for as part of the 2014 restatement, implying net income and receivables were previously overstated.

Recurring pattern of NT 10-K late-filing notices (2009, 2013, 2014) followed by amendments (10-K/A in 2010 and 2014) suggests chronic weaknesses in internal control over financial reporting and closing processes, increasing the likelihood that quarterly/annual expense accruals (SG&A, restructuring, interest) were mis-timed or corrected retroactively across periods, shifting income recognition between quarters/years (e.g., quarterly SG&A jumps from 214.0M (Q1 2012) to 331.1M (Q4 2012) per the restated 10-K/A).

top_signals: Same-week filing of original 10-K (2014-03-19) followed immediately by a 10-K/A (2014-03-20) that materially restated FY2011-FY2013 Assets, Liabilities, Goodwill, Intangibles and Debt figures
Repeated NT 10-K late-filing notices in 2009, 2013 and 2014, plus a prior 10-K/A within days of the original 10-K in 2010, indicating chronic financial-reporting/closing control issues

Accounts receivable growing 16-19% vs. revenue growth of 9-10% in FY2011-FY2012, followed by an abrupt reversal (receivables -19.5% vs revenue +19.3%) in FY2013, consistent with delayed reserve recognition later corrected
Large, acquisition-related jumps in Goodwill (+239%) and Intangibles (+57%) in FY2012 far outpacing revenue growth (+8.7%), increasing scope for purchase-accounting/impairment judgment errors

Persistent and large gap between net income and operating cash flow across all years (structurally expected for a vehicle-fleet business, but compounding difficulty in independently verifying earnings quality)
